topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The doorbell buzzed, a jarring sound in the quiet afternoon. I dragged myself from the worn sofa, the effort of rising feeling monumental. Peering through the peephole, I saw a delivery person hovering awkwardly by the door, holding a medium-sized cardboard box. My shoulders slumped further as I shuffled to the entrance. It was a package, clearly addressed to the former resident, Sarah. A strange sense of obligation settled over me, heavy and unwelcome. I managed a weak nod, my eyes already drifting back toward the relative darkness of the hallway. I just wanted to sink back down.

[story 2]

I hadn't had a proper rest in days. My eyelids felt weighted, and the effort required to simply lift my head felt like hauling a boulder uphill. When the delivery arrived, it was a surprise. A box addressed to the previous occupant. I leaned against the doorframe, letting the cool wood press against my back. The cardboard felt flimsy in my hands. I stared at the label, a faint, dull ache blooming behind my eyes. Why did this intrude? I barely managed to lower the box onto the mat, the movement slow and deliberate, as if my limbs were filled with sand.

[story 3]

The afternoon light filtering through the dusty window seemed too bright. Every movement felt like an unnecessary exertion. When the delivery notification chimed, I hesitated. My gaze drifted toward the box sitting on the threshold. It was meant for someone else. A low groan escaped my lips, a sound more from deep exhaustion than annoyance. I didn't want to engage; the energy required to even open it seemed beyond my current reserves. I just wanted to close the door and find a corner where I could simply cease existing for a while.
